.single-post .entry-content ul,
.single-post .entry-content ol{
  list-style: none !important;
  margin: 1rem 0 1.25rem !important;
  padding-left: 0 !important;
  overflow: visible !important;         /* in case a theme clips ::before */
}

.single-post .entry-content li{
  list-style: none !important;
  position: relative !important;
  padding-left: 1.5rem !important;      /* space for the badge */
  margin: .55rem 0 !important;
  line-height: 1.7 !important;
}

/* Some themes set ::before/::marker globally – neutralize it */
.single-post .entry-content li::marker,
.single-post .entry-content li:before{
  text-indent: 0;
}

/* ==== 2) Unified badge design (check & number use the same look) ==== */
:root{
  --marker-bg: #F8CA13;     /* badge background */
  --marker-fg: #fff;        /* icon/number color */
}

.single-post .entry-content ul li::before,
.single-post .entry-content ol li::before{
  position: absolute !important;
  left: 0 !important;
  top: .35rem !important;
  width: 16px !important;
  height: 16px !important;
  border-radius: 999px !important;
  background: var(--marker-bg) !important;
  color: var(--marker-fg) !important;
  display: inline-flex !important;
  align-items: center !important;
  justify-content: center !important;
  font-weight: 700 !important;
  font-family: Inter, system-ui, Arial, sans-serif !important;
  box-shadow: 0 2px 6px rgba(0,0,0,.12) !important;
  z-index: 0 !important;
  content: "" !important;                 /* will be set per list below */
}

/* UL → check icon */
.single-post .entry-content ul li::before{
  content: "✓" !important;
  font-size: 13px !important;
}

/* OL → number with same badge */
.single-post .entry-content ol{ counter-reset: item; }
.single-post .entry-content ol li::before{
  counter-increment: item;
  content: counter(item) !important;
  font-size: 12px !important;
}

/* Nested lists a bit smaller */
.single-post .entry-content ul ul li,
.single-post .entry-content ol ol li{
  padding-left: 1.9rem !important;
}
.single-post .entry-content ul ul li::before,
.single-post .entry-content ol ol li::before{
  width: 18px !important; height: 18px !important; font-size: 11px !important;
}













/* Disable WooCommerce single product zoom */
.woocommerce-product-gallery__wrapper .zoomImg {
  display: none !important;
}

.woocommerce-product-gallery__image {
  pointer-events: none !important;
}

    border: 2px solid #3851FA;

border-left: 4px solid #2563eb; padding: 16px 20px; margin: 0 0 28px 0; border-radius: 4px;"><p>GoHighLevel snapshots are pre-built sub-account configurations that package workflows, pipelines, funnels, websites, calendars, email templates, SMS sequences, custom fields, and tags into a single shareable file. You import them into a new sub-account and skip 20 to 40 hours of manual setup. Snapshots come in three flavors: free (great for getting started), paid ($97 to $997, niche-specific and high-converting), and agency-built (your own custom systems). This guide covers what snapshots include, what they don’t, how to import them in 15 to 20 minutes, the best free and paid options for 2026, and the common mistakes that cause failed deployments.</p></blockquote><p>You signed up for GoHighLevel, and you’re staring at an empty sub-account. The platform replaces 10+ tools, which means you have a lot to set up.</p><p>This is exactly what snapshots solve.</p><p>A snapshot is a complete sub-account configuration packaged into a shareable link. Click import, and you skip the manual work: workflows, funnels, pipelines, email sequences, SMS templates, calendars, tags, and custom fields all load into your account in under 20 minutes — powered by AutoGen CRM. Visit <strong><a href="https://autogencrm.com/">autogencrm.com</a></strong> to explore ready-made snapshots built for agencies that want to launch faster and smarter.</p><p>Snapshots are how agencies deploy 50 client accounts in the time it would take to build one from scratch. They’re also one of the highest-leverage features in the entire GHL ecosystem.</p><p>This guide covers everything: what snapshots actually include, the three free snapshots most agencies should grab first, the best paid options, how to import properly, and the mistakes that cause silent deployment failures.</p><div style="text-align: center; margin: 32px 0;"><a style="display: inline-block; background: #2563eb; color: #fff; padding: 14px 28px; border-radius: 8px; text-decoration: none; font-weight: bold; font-size: 16px;" href="https://gohighlevel.com/30-day-trial?fp_ref=webspulse" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Try GoHighLevel Free for 30 Days →</a></div><h2>What Are GoHighLevel Snapshots?</h2><p><img data-lazyloaded="1" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHdpZHRoPSIxMjgwIiBoZWlnaHQ9IjcyMCIgdmlld0JveD0iMCAwIDEyODAgNzIwIj48cmVjdCB3aWR0aD0iMTAwJSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxMDAlIiBzdHlsZT0iZmlsbDojY2ZkNGRiO2ZpbGwtb3BhY2l0eTogMC4xOyIvPjwvc3ZnPg==" decoding="async" class="size-full wp-image-10210 aligncenter" data-src="https://autogencrm.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/ghl-snapshots.jpg" alt="" width="1280" height="720" data-srcset="https://autogencrm.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/ghl-snapshots.jpg 1280w, https://autogencrm.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/ghl-snapshots-300x169.jpg 300w, https://autogencrm.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/ghl-snapshots-1024x576.jpg 1024w, https://autogencrm.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/ghl-snapshots-768x432.jpg 768w, https://autogencrm.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/ghl-snapshots-600x338.jpg 600w" data-sizes="(max-width: 1280px) 100vw, 1280px"></p><p>A GoHighLevel snapshot is a packaged copy of a sub-account’s configuration. It captures the structure (not the data) of a working account so it can be deployed to another sub-account without rebuilding from scratch.</p><p>Think of it like a save file for a fully configured GHL setup. Workflows, pipelines, funnels, calendars, email templates, SMS sequences, tags, custom fields, and global settings all bundle into one shareable link.</p><p>When you import the snapshot, GHL recreates that entire setup inside the destination account. You then customize branding, connect integrations, set Custom Values, and you’re live.</p><h3>What Snapshots Include and Don’t Include</h3><p>This is the most important table in this entire article. Most snapshot deployment problems trace back to misunderstanding what gets transferred.</p><table style="width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin: 24px 0;"><thead><tr style="background: #1e40af; color: #fff;"><th style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd; text-align: left;">✅ Included in Snapshot</th><th style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd; text-align: left;">❌ NOT Included in Snapshot</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr style="background: #f9fafb;"><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Workflow automation sequences</td><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Contacts and contact data</td></tr><tr><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Pipeline stages and structure</td><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Calendar availability and connected emails</td></tr><tr style="background: #f9fafb;"><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Funnels and websites (pages and structure)</td><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Stripe, Gmail, and other integration credentials</td></tr><tr><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Email templates and SMS templates</td><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">A2P 10DLC registration (transfers per account)</td></tr><tr style="background: #f9fafb;"><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Forms and surveys</td><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Billing configuration</td></tr><tr><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Custom field definitions</td><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">User accounts and permissions</td></tr><tr style="background: #f9fafb;"><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Tags and Smart Lists</td><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">LC Phone numbers</td></tr><tr><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Custom Values (keys, not values)</td><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Conversation history and message logs</td></tr><tr style="background: #f9fafb;"><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Calendar configurations (structure)</td><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Domain settings</td></tr><tr><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Trigger links</td><td style="padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">Trustpilot or review platform connections</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>The big takeaway:</strong> snapshots transfer structure, not data, not connections, not credentials. After import, you connect integrations, configure Custom Values, and activate workflows manually.</p><h3>The Three Sharing Modes</h3><p>GHL supports three snapshot sharing options. Pick the right one for your situation.</p><p><strong>1. Public Link.</strong> Anyone with the link can import. Use for marketplace listings, free downloads, blog post lead magnets, and community sharing.</p><p><strong>2. Restricted Link.</strong> Only specific GHL accounts can import using the link. Use for premium paid snapshots and partner-only sharing. Buyers receive access after purchase.</p><p><strong>3. Agency-Only.</strong> The snapshot is only deployable within your own agency’s sub-accounts. Not externally shareable. Use this for proprietary agency systems you don’t want distributed publicly.</p><h2>Free GoHighLevel Snapshots Worth Grabbing</h2><p>Free snapshots are perfect for getting started, learning how snapshots work, or solving specific operational problems without building from scratch.</p><p>Three free snapshots that solve real pain points for most agencies and businesses:</p><h3>1. A2P 10DLC Snapshot</h3><p>A pre-configured setup for SMS compliance and A2P 10DLC registration. Saves you hours on the carrier compliance process and helps you avoid the most common SMS-blocking issues.<br>
<a href="https://affiliates.gohighlevel.com/?fp_ref=autogencrm&share=tYrtloGCUJLMlkTx08o5" target="_blank" rel="noopener">👉 Import this snapshot for free</a></p><p><strong>Best for:</strong> Anyone running US SMS for the first time. The A2P process trips up most new GHL users.</p><h3>2. 4-Year Email Nurture Campaign Snapshot</h3><p>A long-term lead nurture sequence with weekly email touchpoints, SMS triggers, and behavioral automation over 4 years. Includes segmentation logic, event-based follow-ups, and re-engagement branches.<br>
<a href="https://affiliates.gohighlevel.com/?fp_ref=autogencrm&share=0hTD9PrDA825EXCEBTPU" target="_blank" rel="noopener">👉 Import this snapshot for free</a></p><p><strong>Best for:</strong> Coaches, consultants, course creators, and agencies who want to keep cold leads warm without manual follow-up. The 4-year horizon catches buyers who weren’t ready in year 1 or 2.</p><h3>3. AI Employee Snapshot</h3><p>Pre-built workflows that use the GHL AI Employee Suite for lead follow-up, task creation, call transcription handling, and engagement scoring. Includes templates for Conversation AI training, Voice AI scripts, and the workflow connections that tie AI features into your CRM properly.<br>
<a href="https://affiliates.gohighlevel.com/?fp_ref=autogencrm&share=bIvlp0JRQdE2PDjUixeP" target="_blank" rel="noopener">👉 Import this snapshot for free</a></p><p><strong>Best for:</strong> Agencies and businesses adopting AI features in 2026 who don’t want to wire up the AI integration manually.</p><div style="background: #f0f7ff; border-left: 4px solid #2563eb; padding: 16px 20px; margin: 24px 0; border-radius: 4px;"><strong>Get Free Snapshots:</strong> The AutoGenCRM snapshot library includes the three free snapshots above plus a few more lead-magnet options. Verify merge fields resolve to placeholders, not blank or broken data.</p><h3>Step 3: Create the Snapshot</h3><p>Agency Dashboard → Snapshots → Create Snapshot → select source sub-account → choose components → name with version number (e.g., “HVAC v4 Apr 2026”) → save.</p><h3>Step 4: Document the Snapshot</h3><p>Write a setup document listing every Custom Value that needs to be filled in after import, every integration to reconnect, and the activation steps. Importing into a live client account.</strong> Always import into a fresh sub-account first, configure it, then migrate specific workflows or pipelines to the live account if needed. Direct import into a live account can overwrite existing pipeline stages and workflow configurations without warning.</p><p><strong>2. Forgetting to activate workflows.</strong> All imported workflows start as Draft. If you forget to activate them, your automations never fire. Don’t overthink it.</p><p><em>Article verified and updated for 2026.</em></p><h3>Frequently Asked Questions</h3><p><strong>What is a GoHighLevel snapshot?</strong></p><p>A GoHighLevel snapshot is a packaged copy of a sub-account’s configuration, including workflows, pipelines, funnels, websites, calendars, email templates, SMS sequences, custom fields, tags, and forms. Snapshots can be shared via link and imported into any GoHighLevel sub-account, making them the fastest way to deploy a complete CRM and marketing system without rebuilding from scratch.</p><p><strong>Are GoHighLevel snapshots free?</strong></p><p>Many snapshots are free, including official options in the GHL Marketplace and community-shared snapshots from agencies. Paid snapshots typically range from $97 to $997 depending on complexity and niche. Creating snapshots within your own agency for your own sub-accounts is included in all GHL plans at no extra charge.</p><p><strong>How long does it take to import a GoHighLevel snapshot?</strong></p><p>The import itself takes 1 to 5 minutes. Post-import configuration (Custom Values, integration reconnection, workflow activation, branding customization, A2P registration) takes another 15 minutes to 3 hours depending on complexity. Plan for 30 to 60 minutes total for most snapshots.</p><p><strong>What does a GoHighLevel snapshot include?</strong></p><p>Snapshots include workflows, pipelines, funnels, websites, calendars (structure), email and SMS templates, custom field definitions, tags, Smart Lists, forms, and trigger links. They do NOT include contacts, integration credentials, A2P 10DLC registration, billing, user accounts, LC Phone numbers, conversation history, or domain settings. Structure transfers; data and credentials don’t.</p><h3>Can I sell my own GoHighLevel snapshots?</h3><p>Yes. Many GHL agencies sell their snapshots through the official Marketplace, third-party sites, or directly to other agencies. Pricing typically ranges from $97 to $997 depending on complexity. A well-built niche snapshot can generate $10,000 to $30,000 in one-time revenue from a single build investment, depending on demand.</p><p><strong>How do I update an existing snapshot?</strong></p><p>Make changes in the source sub-account, then create a new snapshot from Agency Dashboard → Snapshots → Create New Snapshot with a new version number (e.g., “v4 April 2026”). The old snapshot link remains valid for users on the previous version. GHL does not auto-update existing snapshot installations. Distribute the new link manually to anyone who needs the update.</p><p><strong>Why are my workflows in Draft after importing a snapshot?</strong></p><p>This is intentional. GHL sets all imported workflows to Draft status as a safety measure to prevent accidental sends during setup. Go to Automation → Workflows and manually activate each workflow when you’re ready. Without this step, your snapshot’s automations will not fire.</p><h3>Can I import a snapshot into an existing client account?</h3><p>You can, but it’s risky. Naming conflicts can overwrite existing pipeline stages and workflow configurations without warning. Best practice: import into a fresh sub-account first, configure it, then manually migrate only the specific workflows or pipelines you need into the live client account.</p><p><strong>What’s the difference between free and paid GoHighLevel snapshots?</strong></p><p>Free snapshots typically solve one specific problem (A2P setup, email nurture, AI workflows) and work as lead magnets or starter kits. Paid snapshots ($97 to $997) are usually niche-specific (real estate, dental, med spa, SaaS) with complete systems including funnels, workflows, copy, and proven conversion structures. Paid snapshots often include creator support, lifetime updates, and detailed setup documentation.</p><p><strong>Can I share a snapshot publicly?</strong></p><p>Yes.
